For Long Beach, the MAE and MAPE for loaded imports were 23,896 and 8.17%, respectively,"},{"url":"https://www.wmar2news.com/news/region/baltimore-city/port-of-baltimore-has-its-second-best-year-ever-one-year-after-the-key-bridge-collapse","title":"Port of Baltimore bounces back, hits 50 million tons in second-best year ever","content":"BALTIMORE — The Helen Delich Bentley Port of Baltimore handled 50 million tons of cargo in 2025 at its state-owned public and private marine terminals, marking its second-best year ever.
The 2025 total surpassed Baltimore's 45.9 million tons handled in 2024, according to the Governor's office, and would have been even higher had the Francis Scott Key Bridge not been destroyed by the cargo ship Dali in 2024.
Moore said the port carries an economic value of $70 billion and supports more than 273,000 Maryland jobs.
Baltimore ranked 10th among all U.S. ports for foreign cargo value and 11th for foreign cargo tonnage.
The Port handled 2,223 cargo vessel visits in 2025, surpassing the previous record of 2,137 ships set in 2023 — a 21 percent increase over 2024.
In 2025, 413,639 passengers departed on cruises from Baltimore, ranking among the top 10 passenger counts in the Port's cruise history."},{"url":"https://mpa.maryland.gov/Documents/2025FCSRADA.pdf","title":"THE PORT OF BALTIMORE’S WATERBOURNE COMMERCE – 2011","content":"The Port of Baltimore consists of state-owned marine terminals managed by the MPA and privately owned marine terminals.
Approximately 85% of all general cargo tons move through the MPA’s public terminals, while the private terminals handled most of the Port’s bulk commodities.
The POB ranked as the 11th largest US port in foreign cargo tons and the 4th largest on the East Coast."},{"url":"https://governor.maryland.gov/news/press-releases/governor-moore-announces-new-port-baltimore-cargo-records","title":"Governor Moore Announces New Port of Baltimore Cargo Records | The Office of Governor Wes Moore","content":"$80.8 billion in foreign cargo value (previous record was $74.3 billion in 2022)
11.7 million tons of general cargo tons (previous record was 11.3 million tons in 2022)
In addition to the records, the Port of Baltimore handled 847,158 cars and light trucks in 2023, the 13th consecutive year it has led all other U.S. ports in that commodity.
Mid-Bay will eventually replace Poplar Island as the state’s primary receiving site for Chesapeake Bay channel dredged sediment."}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"Port of Baltimore 2024 annual cargo tonnage value ranking US ports","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.ajot.com/premium/ajot-port-of-baltimore-a-remarkable-performance-but-challenges-ahead","title":"Port of Baltimore: A remarkable performance but challenges ahead | AJOT.COM","content":"## *In 2024 the Port of Baltimore overcame a great deal to post the second-best year on record. For eleven long weeks in 2024 the Fort McHenry main ship channel in Baltimore was closed with debris from the allision of the MV Dali with the Francis Scott Key Bridge. It is worth noting that prior to the collision and collapse of the bridge, the Port was likely on its way to a record-breaking year in 2024. The combined Helen Delich Bentley Port of Baltimore’s state-owned public and private marine terminals handled 45.9 million tons of cargo in 2024, making it the second-best ever year after 2023 when the port handled a record 52.3 million tons. Although the Baltimore’s container business and Ro/Ro figures attract a lot of ink, it is easy to argue that bulk and breakbulk cargo are equally essential to the Port of Baltimore’s tonnage success, resulting in the Port’s total of 45.9 million tons and an eighth place ranking nationally in 2024.","score":0.9436445,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.facebook.com/baltimoreport/posts/big-news-governor-wes-moore-announced-today-that-the-port-of-baltimore-handled-4/1033597325468662","title":"Port of Baltimore","content":"BIG NEWS: Governor Wes Moore announced today that the Port of Baltimore handled 45.9 million tons of cargo in 2024, marking the second-best","score":0.91671574,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://interactive.savills.us/ports-report-2024/p/2","title":"2024 Savills Ports Report - Industrial","content":"TEU volume was up 4.9% in 2023, making Baltimore the only major U.S port to see a rise in activity from 2022.
